# Build ROCm from source

# Build ROCm from source[\#](#build-rocm-from-source "Link to this heading")

[ !(data:image/svg+xml;base64,) ] 2026-01-23

[ !(data:image/svg+xml;base64,) ] 3 min read time

Applies to Linux and Windows

ROCm is an open-source stack from which you can build from source code. The source code is available from [ROCm/ROCm](https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm).

The general steps to build ROCm are:

1.  Clone the ROCm source code

2.  Prepare the build environment

3.  Run the build command

Because the ROCm stack is constantly evolving, the most current instructions are stored with the source code in GitHub. For detailed build instructions, see [Getting and Building ROCm from Source](https://github.com/ROCm/ROCm?tab=readme-ov-file#getting-and-building-rocm-from-source).
